compiled_state.title: MCP fsck(recover=True) runs the destructive recovery path under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
MCP fsck tool accepts recover=True under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1 and runs the destructive recovery script.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1; build_server().
2. call_tool("fsck", {"recover": true}).
3. ticket-fsck-recover.sh executes (returned "No stale rebase...nothing to recover" — it ran).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: recovery (git rebase --continue/--abort, cherry-picks, commits) blocked when readonly.
- Actual: callable; executes the destructive path. The script's own header: "IMPORTANT — this script IS destructive. It modifies git state."

## Source
- src/rebar/mcp_server.py:204-208 — fsck declared in the ungated read section with a recover param.
- src/rebar/__init__.py:369-371 — recover=True -> fsck-recover.
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket-fsck-recover.sh:12-16 — destructive warning.

## Fix direction
Reject recover=True under _readonly(), or split a write-gated fsck_recover tool. Coordinate with bug ship-mogul-glob (env parse) — same patch area.

## Suggested RED test
Readonly server + fsck(recover=True) -> ToolError; non-readonly recover still works; plain fsck() unaffected in both.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] fsck(recover=True) blocked under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1
- [ ] Plain fsck() (non-destructive) remains available readonly
- [ ] RED test first, GREEN after

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (validated 2026-06-09): validated guard is in the fsck tool body:
    if recover and _readonly():
        raise ValueError("fsck recover=True is a write operation and is disabled: this server is read-only (REBAR_MCP_READONLY)")
Validated together with the case-insensitive _readonly() fix on bug ship-mogul-glob (28d8) — land them together; engine confirmed never invoked under any truthy READONLY spelling. test_mcp.py + test_surface.py: 11 passed with both patches.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
